name: Interior Archetype - House
required_conditions: The interior is a single room, not a multi-room house or a multi-floor residence.
The interior has exactly one primary entrance/exit.
The interior's function is domestic dwelling, not commerce, lodging-for-hire, or a notable NPC's seat of authority.
The interior is reached from a town or village exterior map.

provenance.notes: Confidence inherited directly from the source pattern: two independent official sample maps (Map018.json, Map019.json) corroborate every Layout, Passability, and Event rule, and agree on the composition finding that matters here (low density, single back-wall shelf run). modules_required intentionally sets MOD-COUNTER-SERVICE-POINT max_count to 0 -- a house has no commercial service point; the row exists only so a citing implementation contract cannot accidentally attach one without an explicit archetype revision. WO-0059 candidate not authored: a zoned/expanded (multi-room residence) tier was considered and explicitly rejected, not omitted -- PAT-INTERIOR-HOUSE's Required Conditions forbid 'a multi-room house or a multi-floor residence' outright (see chief-house.pattern.md for the actual multi-floor case, a distinct archetype this work order does not author). Building a zoned/expanded ARCH-INTERIOR-HOUSE layout family would violate that condition, not just carry low confidence.
